motivation: Let users add and remove note tags in the v2 UI while keeping backend/Pydantic as the single normalization and validation authority.

included: a compact Vue tag editor for the active note that emits raw add/remove intent
backend test coverage that pins update-time tag normalization, deduplication, sorting, and invalid-tag rejection
frontend tests that pin raw UpdateNoteRequest.tags pass-through in services and useNotes
AppShell wiring that persists add/remove tag actions through the existing note update API and trusts the returned NoteDTO
full UI/backend regression verification after the tag editing flow lands
excluded: frontend normalization, lowercasing, sorting, regex validation, or deduplication of tag values before save
backend production code, schemas, models, routes, migrations, or new tag endpoints; PUT /api/notes/{id} already supports full tag replacement
partial tag add/remove endpoints, a global tag-list endpoint, server-side tag filtering, tag colors, tag ordering, and bulk tag administration
new dependencies, router or Pinia adoption, or third-party icon libraries
design-token additions or hand edits to generated ui/src/design-system/tokens.css
unrelated documentation drift cleanup for previously-landed note create/edit/delete/tag persistence work

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Build compact raw-intent note tag editor
work_items.design_reference: app shell edit pane tag editor

component_map.mapping: Custom presentational Vue SFC under ui/src/features/notes
component_map.notes: Typed props and emits; no HTTP calls; props down and raw add/remove events up.
No new dependency and no new design-system abstraction unless existing DsToolButton is useful for icon-sized controls.

props.error: optional string | null for backend mutation errors

emits.add-tag: raw string exactly as entered by the user
emits.remove-tag: existing backend-normalized chip value selected for removal

behavior: renders current backend-returned tags as stable-keyed chips with accessible remove buttons labelled with each tag
keeps the tag input as local UI state and emits add-tag with the raw entered string on Enter or add-button click
blocks only an obviously blank add action by checking trimmed length for UI affordance; it does not trim the emitted value
does not lowercase, remove leading #, collapse whitespace, sort, deduplicate, regex-validate, or otherwise normalize tag values
does not mutate props and does not call HTTP APIs
disables input and buttons while disabled is true and can display backend mutation errors passed by the parent
uses scoped styles and existing semantic CSS variables only

signals: contract-mutation:additive Vue component interface for NoteTagEditor props and add-tag/remove-tag emits
explicit-open-question:frontend intentionally does not normalize or validate tags beyond blank-submit UI affordance

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/features/notes/NoteTagEditor.test.ts
assertions: NoteTagEditor emits add-tag with the raw string, including case, spaces, and a leading # when present.
NoteTagEditor emits remove-tag with the selected existing chip value.
Obviously blank input does not emit add-tag, but nonblank values are not lowercased, deduplicated, sorted, regex-checked, or otherwise normalized in the component.
The component uses typed props/emits, stable v-for keys, scoped styles, PlusIcon, and XIcon.

consumes.schemas: backend/app/schemas/schemas.py NoteUpdate.tags uses the backend normalize_tags validator
consumes.router: backend/app/routers/notes.py PUT /api/notes/{note_id} replaces tags only after Pydantic validation

behavior: PUT /api/notes/{id} with raw duplicate tags such as ' Work ', '#work', and 'Daily Log' returns normalized, deduplicated, sorted tags from the backend
PUT /api/notes/{id} with invalid tag values returns 422 and does not change the existing tag set
PUT /api/notes/{id} with omitted tags still preserves the previous tag set and tags: [] still clears it

signals: contract-mutation:test coverage pins backend as the sole normalization and validation boundary

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/notes/test_note_tags_api.py tests/unit/schemas/test_schemas.py -v
assertions: Backend update tests prove raw duplicate tag inputs are normalized, deduplicated, and sorted by the backend.
Backend update tests prove invalid tag values return 422 and preserve the stored tag set.
Existing preserve-on-omit and clear-with-empty-array tests still pass.

work_items.title: Pin frontend raw tag update pass-through

consumes.service: ui/src/services/notes.ts updateNote(noteId, payload) and UpdateNoteRequest.tags?: string[] | null
consumes.composable: ui/src/features/notes/useNotes.ts updateNote(noteId, payload) maps the returned NoteDTO into the matching NoteMock

behavior: updateNote(noteId, { tags: [...] }) sends the tags array unchanged, including raw case, spaces, and leading # characters
updateNote(noteId, { tags: [] }) sends an empty array so the backend clears tags
content-only updates keep tags omitted from the PUT body
useNotes.updateNote replaces the matching local NoteMock.tags with backend-returned NoteDTO.tags, including normalized tags and the empty-array clear case

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/notes.test.ts src/features/notes/useNotes.test.ts
assertions: services/notes.test covers updateNote with a raw non-empty tags array and verifies the JSON body is unchanged.
services/notes.test covers updateNote with tags: [] and keeps the existing content-only update payload assertion without tags.
useNotes.test covers updateNote mapping backend-normalized returned DTO tags back into the local notes ref, including clearing tags to [].

work_items.title: Wire raw tag add/remove actions into AppShell

component_map.notes: Owns persistence through useNotes.updateNote; no router or global store added.
Rendered in edit mode near the existing NoteEditor markdown form.
Remain filter-only; tag mutation happens through the edit-mode tag editor.

consumes.component: NoteTagEditor add-tag raw strings and remove-tag existing chip values
consumes.backend: existing PUT /api/notes/{note_id} full-replacement tag semantics: omitted/null preserves, [] clears, array replaces and backend normalizes

behavior: AppShell renders NoteTagEditor in edit mode using activeNote.tags from the backend-returned note
handleAddTag(rawTag) sends persistNoteUpdate(activeNote.id, { tags: [...activeNote.tags, rawTag] }) without frontend normalization or deduplication
handleRemoveTag(tag) sends persistNoteUpdate(activeNote.id, { tags: activeNote.tags.filter((existingTag) => existingTag !== tag) }); removing the last tag sends tags: []
successful tag add/remove uses the returned NoteDTO through useNotes mapping, so visible chips, sidebar cards, and tag filter counts reflect backend-normalized tags
failed tag add/remove leaves the saved note unchanged and surfaces mutationError through the existing action-error/tag-editor error path
content save remains content-focused: markdown Save keeps omitting tags when tag actions are not part of the payload
after a successful removal, AppShell prunes activeTags values that no longer exist in allTags(notes) so stale invisible filters cannot hide the current note
read-mode active-note tag buttons and SidebarDrawer TagFilter remain filter-only

signals: contract-mutation:AppShell adds raw tag add/remove update actions using UpdateNoteRequest.tags
blast-radius:touches the top-level shell state machine and its integration-style test file
capability-escalation:user-visible persisted tag writes through the existing notes update network path
explicit-open-question:tag add/remove persists immediately through the editor and is not part of markdown draft save/cancel semantics

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts
assertions: AppShell edit mode displays the tag editor seeded from activeNote.tags.
Adding a raw tag such as ' Daily Log ' sends a PUT body containing the raw value unchanged and no content field.
The mocked backend response can return normalized tags such as ['daily-log', ...], and AppShell renders the returned normalized tags in the read pane, sidebar card, and tag filter.
Removing a tag sends the remaining complete tag set; removing the last tag sends tags: [].
Content-only markdown saves preserve the existing content-only payload behavior and omit tags.
A failed backend tag add/remove keeps the saved note unchanged and shows the existing mutation error alert.
After a successful removal, stale active tag filters are pruned when no remaining note has that tag.
Read-mode tag filter interactions covered by existing tests still pass.

work_items.title: Run full tag editing regression gate

verifies: the merged binder result clears the repo full gate
backend tag persistence tests remain green without backend production changes
no new dependency is required for add/remove tag editing
contract.source_changes: No feature changes expected; only small test fixture or assertion corrections are allowed if the full gate exposes drift.

assertions: Full UI and backend gate passes after tag editing changes are merged.
Existing backend note tag persistence coverage remains green.
The final diff adds no third-party dependency for this feature.
